title: Noun たる Noun
titleEn: Indicates 'being in the position/state of', used for roles or statuses.
explanation: Indicates 'being in the position/state of', used for roles or statuses. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun1 たる Noun2」expresses that the second noun is in the 'role/state/condition' of the first. It often has a formal or literary tone, emphasizing that someone/something rightly holds or embodies that status.

title: Noun ですら
titleEn: Means 'even', 'as much as', used for emphasis in formal contexts.
explanation: Means 'even', 'as much as', used for emphasis in formal contexts. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun + ですら」emphasizes 'even' in a formal or literary tone. It can carry nuance similar to さえ or すら but is used more in writing or formal speech.

title: Noun でなくてなんだろう
titleEn: A strong rhetorical question: 'If not X, then what is it?'
structure: Noun + でなくてなんだろう
structureEn: Noun + でなくてなんだろう
explanation: A strong rhetorical question: 'If not X, then what is it?' (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun + でなくてなんだろう」expresses the speaker’s strong conviction that something must be that noun—no other possibility. Literally, 'If it isn't Noun, then what is it?'

title: Noun ではあるまいし
titleEn: Means 'it’s not as if...', 'it’s not that...', more formal than じゃあるまいし.
explanation: Means 'it’s not as if...', 'it’s not that...', more formal than じゃあるまいし. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun + ではあるまいし」indicates that the noun is not the situation or reason being implied. It dismisses a possibility or justification, e.g. 'It’s not as if X, so don’t worry/do that/etc.' This is the more formal counterpart to じゃあるまいし.

title: Noun と Noun を兼ねて
titleEn: Used to indicate doing two things at once or fulfilling two roles/purposes.
structure: Noun + と + Noun + を兼ねて
structureEn: Noun + と + Noun + を兼ねて
explanation: Used to indicate doing two things at once or fulfilling two roles/purposes. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: 「Noun と Noun を兼ねて」means that one action or thing serves the purpose of both nouns. It can be translated as 'serving as both… and…', or 'for both… and…'.

title: Noun といい Noun といい
titleEn: Used to express 'both A and B'; 'whether it's A or B'; 'not to mention A and also B'.
structure: Noun1 + といい + Noun2 + といい + Comment
structureEn: Noun1 + といい + Noun2 + といい + Comment
explanation: Used to express 'both A and B'; 'whether it's A or B'; 'not to mention A and also B'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: This grammar structure is used to provide two examples or two situations to demonstrate a certain characteristic or situation. It's a way of mentioning two items of the same theme to generalize or emphasize something applicable to them both. In English, it can be understood as 'both A and B', 'whether it's A or B', or 'not to mention A and also B'.

title: Noun という Noun
titleEn: Used to specify or define one noun with another; 'the noun called', 'the noun that is', or 'a certain'.
explanation: Used to specify or define one noun with another; 'the noun called', 'the noun that is', or 'a certain'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The Noun という Noun construction is used to specify or define one noun with another. It usually translates as 'the noun called', 'the noun that is' or simply 'a certain'. It is a very common pattern for clarifying what something is named or known as.

title: Noun というところだ
titleEn: Means 'it's approximately...' or 'it's about...'. Used to show approximation or a general assessment.
explanation: Means 'it's approximately...' or 'it's about...'. Used to show approximation or a general assessment. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The というところだ grammar point is used to show an approximation or general assessment of something. It is often translated as 'it's approximately...', 'around...', or 'it's about...'.

title: Noun + というもの
titleEn: Used to express a generic concept: 'all ~', 'any ~ of that kind'.
explanation: Used to express a generic concept: 'all ~', 'any ~ of that kind'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar pattern 'Noun + というもの' refers to the entirety or general nature of that noun—often implying 'all of this category' or 'this thing called...'. It can also highlight a universal truth about the noun mentioned.

title: Noun といったところだ
titleEn: Expresses 'at most', 'as much as', or 'limited to'.
structure: Noun + といったところだ
structureEn: Noun + といったところだ
explanation: Expresses 'at most', 'as much as', or 'limited to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: Noun といったところだ describes an upper limit or a maximum extent, often translating to 'at most' or 'around...'. It suggests the speaker doesn’t expect anything beyond that limit.
